summary: I audited the technical design (`design-writer/output/design.md`) against the canonical business requirements (`requirements-writer/output/requirements.md`, R1–R18) and emitted a **needs_work** verdict. The design covers surface placement, live Turbo broadcasting, load-time truth, out-of-order convergence, and accessibility well (R1–R3, R7, R8, R10, R13–R18). But because the design writer's `input.json` had `resolved_inputs: []`, it derived its own looser R1–R6 from the ask and missed six granular behaviors the real requirements specify: R4 (hide the iteration number on the first attempt — the design always appends "iteration N"), R5 (name both steps when two are active — the design names one plus "+N more running"), R6 (collapse three-plus to "<Phase>: N steps are running" — same mismatched mechanism), R9 (name where a failed pipeline stopped — the "Aborted" branch has no location), R11 (distinct Paused/Canceled wording — no such branch), and R12 (a guaranteed never-blank catch-all for uncovered/future states — no explicit default terminal fallback). I also flagged one minor over-design item (the unrequested `as_of` timestamp). Seven findings total; verdict written to `verdict.json`.
